sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Optimism Fuels Rally

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally on Mideast Peace Hopes and SpaceX IPO
items.summary: U.S. stock indices concluded the week on a significantly positive note (June 12, 2026), driven by optimism surrounding a potential Middle East peace framework involving the U.S., Iran, and Israel, which is expected to ease geopolitical tensions. Investor sentiment also received a substantial boost from the historic debut of SpaceX's initial public offering. The 'fear index' (VIX) declined, signaling an improved risk appetite among investors.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars in Historic Public Debut
items.summary: SpaceX ($SPCX) made its highly anticipated debut on the Nasdaq, with shares soaring and closing up 19.22% at $160.95 on its first trading day. The successful IPO reinforced confidence in equity markets.

items.headline: Powerlaw Corp. Surges on SpaceX Hype
items.summary: Powerlaw Corp. ($PWRL), a closed-end fund with SpaceX as its largest holding (approximately 19% of net assets), surged Friday pre-market, benefiting from the SpaceX IPO euphoria.

items.headline: Albemarle Rises with Lithium Prices
items.summary: Albemarle ($ALB) saw a significant gain of 7.40% as lithium prices, which had recently fallen, began to rise again.

items.headline: Mosaic Company Boosted by EU Regulation Ease
items.summary: Mosaic Company ($MOS) climbed 7.56% after the European Union agreed to ease regulations within the fertilizer industry.

items.headline: Broadcom Plummets Post-Earnings
items.summary: Broadcom ($AVGO) shares dropped 13% following disappointing earnings results that fell short of market expectations, impacting the broader semiconductor sector.

items.headline: Adobe Falls Amid CFO Departure News
items.summary: Adobe ($ADBE) saw a notable decline of 6.8% despite reporting stronger-than-expected profit and revenue, with news of its Chief Financial Officer's departure weighing on the stock.

items.headline: Eli Lilly Drops Amid Healthcare Sector Weakness
items.summary: Eli Lilly ($LLY) was among several pharmaceutical stocks experiencing broad weakness, contributing to the healthcare sector's underperformance with a 2.39% drop.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Sees Inflows into Defensives and Basic Materials
items.summary: Recent market movements indicate a shift, with Consumer Discretionary and Financials leading over the past month. For June 12, 2026, the Materials sector was the top performer, gaining 1.83%. Basic Materials, Blockchain, and Consumer Staples have attracted inflows, while the Healthcare sector faced the largest drawdown (-0.16% on June 12) and continues to lag with a year-to-date loss of -3.86%. Technology, despite strong trailing relative strength, shows signs of 'rolling over.'

items.headline: Fed Meeting Next Week: Focus on New Leadership and 'Dot Plot'
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is scheduled for June 16-17, marking new Chairman Kevin Warsh's first meeting. While markets largely anticipate a hold on interest rates, investor attention will be on the updated 'dot plot' projections for future rate paths and Warsh's initial guidance, particularly regarding accelerating inflation and labor market stability.

items.headline: PPI Shows Elevated Wholesale Inflation; Consumer Sentiment Improves
items.summary: The May Producer Price Index (PPI) revealed a 1.1% increase, pushing the 12-month wholesale inflation rate to 6.5%, the highest level since November 2022. Concurrently, the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index for June unexpectedly rose to 48.9 (from 44.8 in May), primarily due to easing gasoline prices, although inflation concerns remain elevated.

items.headline: Oil Prices Ease on Hopes of US-Iran Peace Deal
items.summary: Brent crude oil prices declined by 3.4% to $87.33 per barrel (June 12, 2026), deepening weekly losses, as reports indicated significant progress towards a potential US-Iran peace framework. An agreement could ease geopolitical tensions and potentially reopen the Strait of Hormuz, affecting global oil supply.
